title: container does not show exposed ports from the pod when created via --pod-id-file
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**
`podman generate systemd` does not pass on opened ports from the pod to the containers inside the pod -> containers are not reachable from outside. This does not happen when creating the pod by hand.
<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. create a pod: `podman pod create --name testpod -p 2000:2000/tcp`

2. add a container to the pod `podman run --pod testpod --rm -d docker.io/library/alpine:latest /bin/sleep 1000`

3. run `podman ps`: you will see that port 2000:2000/tcp is available to both the pod and the container

4. create systemd units with `podman generate systemd --new -f -n testpod `

5. restart the pod using the freshly created systemd units using `systemctl --user start pod-testpod.service`

6. run `podman ps` again: you will see that only the pod has port 2000:2000/tcp, and the container has no exposed port 

**Describe the results you received:**
When initially creating the pod, both the pod and the container have port 2000:2000/tcp exposed.
When generating a systemd unit and running the pod as systemd user service, then only the pod has port 2000:2000/tcp exposed.

**Describe the results you expected:**
The container has port 2000:2000/tcp exposed as well when run via systemd user service

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.3.0
API Version:  3.3.0
Go Version:   go1.16.6
Built:        Sat Aug 21 04:36:14 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes  (I ran `dnf update --refresh --enablerepo=updates-testing podman`, so according to the install instructions I now have the very latest podman. Will try again when there is something new available in updates-testing, koji is too slow to download from at the moment)

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body: Can you try with 3.3.1, just released? I recall backporting a fix from @Luap99 related to `--pod-id-file`

Reproducer:
```
$ rm -f /tmp/pod.id
$ podman pod create --pod-id-file /tmp/pod.id --name testpod -p 2000:2000/tcp
$ podman run --pod-id-file /tmp/pod.id --rm -d docker.io/library/alpine:latest /bin/sleep 1000
$ podman ps ...
```

> Can you try with 3.3.1, just released? I recall backporting a fix from @Luap99 related to `--pod-id-file`

Good catch, it is fixed in the main branch, closing :)

Works great for me, thanks.
Sorry, I did not understand that it's related to `--pod-id-file` and just looked for systemd stuff in the 3.3.1 release notes.
